No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to health and well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course directly provides quality tertiary education in Chinese language and grammar, promoting literacy and advanced learning, aligning with Targets 4.3 and 4.6.
evidence: 學習謀篇論述的基本寫作能力

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Acquiring advanced language and writing skills can indirectly enhance employability and prospects for decent work (Target 8.5), as inferred by Model B.
evidence: 學習謀篇論述的基本寫作能力

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course caters to diverse proficiency levels (A1-B2), promoting social inclusion by equipping students with language skills for better communication and integration (Target 10.2).

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course objective '人與社會：從日常會話到時事新聞閱讀' (reading current events news) indirectly supports public access to information (Target 16.10).
evidence: 人與社會：從日常會話到時事新聞閱讀

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Teaching Chinese language and linguistics, especially to an international student body and using international academic references, fosters international knowledge sharing and cross-cultural communication (Target 17.6).
evidence: Charles Ting-Chi Tang. 1972. A case grammar of spoken Chinese.
